citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The hard surfaces of the primary enclosure and food and water receptacles for two spider monkeys are being cleaned
with soap and water daily but have not been sanitized. Not sanitizing these areas and items properly creates risk for
illness and/or disease. Hard surfaces of the primary enclosure and food and water receptacles must be kept clean and
must be sanitized at least once every 2 weeks using one of the following methods: 1) live steam under pressure, or 2)
washing with hot water (at least 180 degrees Fahrenheit) and soap or detergent, or 3) washing all soiled surfaces with
appropriate detergent/disinfectant product that accomplishes the same purpose, with a thorough cleaning of the surfaces
to remove organic material first, and then to provide sanitization followed by a clean water rinse, or as often as necessary
to provide for the health and well-being of the animals.
Correct by: 04/15/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.84(b)(3) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The hard surfaces of the primary enclosure and food and water receptacles for two spider monkeys are being cleaned 
with soap and water daily but have not been sanitized. Not sanitizing these areas and items properly creates risk for 
illness and/or disease. Hard surfaces of the primary enclosure and food and water receptacles must be kept clean and 
must be sanitized at least once every 2 weeks using one of the following methods: 1) live steam under pressure, or 2) 
washing with hot water (at least 180 degrees Fahrenheit) and soap or detergent, or 3) washing all soiled surfaces with 
appropriate detergent/disinfectant product that accomplishes the same purpose, with a thorough cleaning of the surfaces 
to remove organic material first, and then to provide sanitization followed by a clean water rinse, or as often as necessary

Correct by: 04/15/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
